verse_text: So Moses said to God, 'Indeed, when I go to the children of Israel and say to them, 'The God of your fathers sent me to you,' and they ask me, 'What is His name?' what shall I tell them?'

links.context: I AM (Gr. ego eimi) is the divine Name of God in the OT, first revealed to Moses at the burning bush ([[EXO.3:13]]-15). To the Jews, this was a direct, explicit, and unmistakable claim to perfect equality with God, as evidenced by their reaction (v. 59; see also Mk 14:62-64). John places special emphasis on the use of this Name to clearly reveal Christ as God. This divine claim illuminates Christ's authority even over death (v. 52), a power belonging only to God the Father.

symbolic.title: Returning to the Garden of Eden
